findings.issue: Task 7 (Code Audit) uses Skill `code-reviewing`, which is not in the Execution Skills catalog (`code-writing`, `infrastructure-setup`, `deploy-pipeline`, `documentation-writing`, `skill-master`, `pre-deploy-qa`, `post-deploy-qa`, `prompt-master`). `code-reviewer` is a Reviewer Agent name, not an Execution Skill.
findings.fix: If audit tasks are intended to be executed by a reviewer agent, model them as reviewers attached to the relevant implementation tasks (Tasks 1-6) rather than standalone tasks; or use an existing execution skill (e.g. `code-writing`) and put the reviewer in `Reviewers:`. Alternatively, drop standalone audit tasks — Reviewers per implementation task already cover this audit dimension and pre-deploy-qa (Task 10) consolidates the rest.

findings.issue: Task 8 (Security Audit) uses Skill `security-auditor`, which is a Reviewer Agent name, not an Execution Skill from the catalog.
findings.fix: Either remove the standalone audit task (Tasks 1-6 already list `security-auditor` as a per-task reviewer, so a separate full-feature audit duplicates work) or restructure as a `pre-deploy-qa` step that aggregates the per-task reviewer outputs.

findings.issue: Task 9 (Test Audit) uses Skill `test-master`, which is not in the Execution Skills catalog. `test-reviewer` is the standard reviewer agent; `test-master` is a planning skill, not an execution skill.
findings.fix: Drop the standalone test audit (per-task `test-reviewer` reviewers + Task 10 pre-deploy-qa cover it) or fold it into Task 10's pre-deploy-qa scope. If kept, choose a Skill from the execution catalog and list `test-reviewer` under Reviewers.

findings.issue: Frontmatter `branch: phase/3.6-bugfix` deviates from the validator template, which expects `branch: dev`. The user has explicitly flagged this as an accepted project convention (CLAUDE.md uses `phase/N-name` for active work, `main` as default). Recording for traceability — not a blocker.
findings.fix: No action required given the explicit project-level override. If alignment with the template is desired, update either the frontmatter or the tech-spec template, but do not block on this.

findings.issue: Task 5 (documentation update of `patterns.md`) lists only `code-reviewer` as Reviewer. A dedicated `documentation-reviewer` agent exists in `.claude/agents/` and is the natural fit for documentation-writing tasks.
findings.fix: Add `documentation-reviewer` to Task 5 Reviewers (e.g. `Reviewers: code-reviewer, documentation-reviewer`).

findings.issue: Length-bounding values `500` (description) and `200` (cause) appear in both Decision 5 and Task 2 description. Per task-quality §8c, configuration values should not be duplicated between Decisions and task descriptions.
findings.fix: In Task 2 description, replace the explicit `(description ≤500, cause ≤200)` with a reference like `(existing length-bounding per Decision 5)`. Keep the numbers in the Decision section only.

findings.issue: Wave 2 description says `Tasks 3, 6, 7 in parallel — disjoint file sets`, but Task 6 is in Wave 3 and Task 7 is the Code Audit (Audit Wave). The intended Wave 2 set is Tasks 3, 4, 5.
findings.fix: Edit the Wave 2 lead-in sentence to read `Tasks 3, 4, 5 in parallel — disjoint file sets`.

summary: Round-2 validation: wave conflicts resolved correctly — `ChatViewModel.kt` is now serialised between Task 3 (Wave 2) and Task 6 (Wave 3); within each wave file sets are disjoint. Frontmatter, structure, decision placement, task brevity, and 10-task count are all in order. Three critical findings remain on the Audit Wave: Tasks 7/8/9 use Skill values (`code-reviewing`, `security-auditor`, `test-master`) that are not in the Execution Skills catalog — they look like reviewer-agent names misused as execution skills. Plus three minor issues: Wave 2 lead-in mislabels the parallel task set, Task 5 lacks `documentation-reviewer`, and length-bound values `500/200` are duplicated between Decision 5 and Task 2.
